avoid_months: end of October (cold increases, water sources freeze, trekking becomes untenable)
snow_or_rain_notes: May (snow on trail after Kedar Kharak and in shaded patches before Kedar Kharak)
From October, precipitation falls as snowfall at higher camps (3-4 inches per snowfall)
Monsoon arrives by end of June, causing landslides and rockfalls on trail
temperature_notes: Summer day: 15–20 °C; night near 0 °C; drops below 10 °C with rain/snow
Autumn day: up to 20 °C; nights can fall below 0 °C; overall temperatures fall 3–5 °C every couple of weeks as season progresses

terrain_challenges: Steep ascent right from the start of the trek, gaining 2,000 ft at a climb of nearly 45-50 degrees
Spider wall section: a steep rock face at almost 90 degrees, traversed on the first day
Rockfall zone within 400 m of starting the second day, a scree section requiring a steep descent
River crossing soon after the rockfall zone on the second day
Extremely steep final ascent to the glacial lake on the summit day
altitude_or_weather_risks: Gangotri basecamp is at 10,050 ft; trek reaches up to 16,000 ft
Risk of Acute Mountain Sickness (AMS) on this trek even with an acclimatisation day
First day alone adds another 3,000 ft of gain from Gangotri
At altitudes above 10,000 ft, AMS risk is real for both first-time and experienced trekkers
Even fit trekkers can develop AMS if they climb too high too soon

fitness_benchmark: Walk, jog or run 10 km in under 60 minutes (minimum recommended fitness level)
approval_requirements: Fitness approval from the Indiahikes team is required 20 days before the trek date; without it you will not be allowed on the trek
At least 3 runs from the last 1 month must meet the fitness criteria
Use apps like Nike or Strava to record runs; profile link must be pasted on Indiahikes account and profile must be public
Fitness record may be rejected if you fail to link your fitness profile or do not meet the required fitness level at least three times in the past month
prep_window: 6 to 8 weeks before the trek begins
6-week plan provided for those not currently active

experience_themes: Frozen Kedartal lake in early summer
Snow on trail after Kedar Kharak in May
Snow plumes on mountain peaks in autumn
Golden/yellow silver birch trees and autumn-coloured shrubs at Kedar Kharak in autumn
Bharal (mountain goat) sightings more likely in autumn
Clear mountain views of Mt Thalaysagar and Mt Bhrigunath in autumn
primary_watchouts: Summer: volatile weather, possible snow even in summer, campsites can turn white with snow
End of June: monsoon-related landslides and rockfalls
Autumn: lack of reliable water sources on trail (sources dry up); carry 2–3 litres of water
End of October: cold increases manifold, water sources freeze, trekking becomes untenable
Trekkers averse to volatile weather and possible summer snowfall
Trekkers unwilling to manage monsoon-related landslide/rockfall risk at end of June
best_for: Trekkers seeking a frozen high-altitude lake and snow on trail (early summer)
Trekkers seeking clear mountain views, autumn foliage, snow plumes, and possible Bharal sightings (autumn)
not_ideal_for: Trekkers averse to volatile weather and possible summer snowfall
Trekkers unwilling to manage monsoon-related landslide/rockfall risk at end of June
Trekkers unable to carry sufficient water on dry autumn trail
Trekkers unprepared for untenable late-October conditions (frozen water sources, severe cold)
